fields.description:
## Context / Problem

Ticket 4af8 injected the vendor mapper entrypoints, but four core modules still reach the
Jira adapter for *scalar* concerns: `applier.py:603-605` (imports `adapters.jira.acli_subprocess`
and calls `resolve_jira_settings(project_default="DIG").project` for the cross-project safety
guard), `fetcher.py:379-381` and `fetcher.py:441-443` (`resolve_jira_settings().project`,
un-defaulted, for JQL scoping and parent enrichment), `_attestation.py:65-79`
(`resolve_jira_settings(project_default="DIG")` + the JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN
fail-fast), and `apply_handlers.py:39` (`from ‚Ä¶adapters.jira.acli_subprocess import
AssigneeNotFoundError`). Epic bbf1 AC3 requires zero `adapters.jira` imports in core.

An earlier version of this ticket also moved the vendor-shaped field-diff helpers
(`diff_fields`/`extract_field`/`assignee_matches`/`local_type_to_remote`) onto the Backend
port; plan review blocked that (correctly) because those helpers are parameterized on Jira
snapshot dicts, violating ADR 0035 ¬ß(d) ("core owns diff/apply; the port only translates").
That concern is now owned by the sibling canonical-shape differ stories under epic bbf1 ‚Äî
this ticket is re-scoped to the scalar, translation-only port surface that the block never
applied to.

## Approach

Extend the `Backend` Protocol in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py` with
scalar, read-only members ‚Äî none parameterized on vendor shapes:

- `project` ‚Äî the project/scope key with the applier's default semantics (Jira:
  `resolve_jira_settings(project_default="DIG").project`), consumed by the `applier.py:601`
  cross-project guard.
- `query_project` ‚Äî the un-defaulted variant (Jira: `resolve_jira_settings().project`),
  preserving the fetcher's fail-closed behavior when unset, consumed at `fetcher.py:379-381`
  and `fetcher.py:441-443`.
- `assert_env_ready()` ‚Äî raises listing missing connection essentials; JiraBackend carries the
  current `_attestation.py:72-79` JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN check.
- A neutral `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError` exception defined in `_backend.py`;
  `adapters/jira/acli_subprocess.py`'s `AssigneeNotFoundError` subclasses it, and
  `apply_handlers.py` catches the neutral base (soft-fail semantics unchanged).

Route the four modules through the backend instance already in scope where one exists,
otherwise through the registry seam (`select_backend(load_config())` ‚Äî the exact pattern
already proven at `outbound_differ.py:450-454`), and delete their
`adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` imports. Extend
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py` with an AST assertion (module-level
AND function-nested) that these four modules import nothing from `adapters.jira`.

Rejected alternative: extending the port with the vendor-shaped diff helpers ‚Äî blocked by
plan review as an ADR 0035 ¬ß(d) violation (a GitHub backend would have to implement
`extract_field(jira_fields, ‚Ä¶)`); the differ is made neutral by the canonical-shape sibling
stories instead. Salvage note: Gerrit change 920 (worktree `impl-4af8-differ-di`) implemented
a superset of this ticket and passed LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1; cherry-pick only the scalar
subset from it, dropping the port field helpers.

## Scope

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py (port: project/query_project/assert_env_ready + BackendAssigneeNotFoundError)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py (JiraBackend implements the new members)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/acli_subprocess.py (AssigneeNotFoundError subclasses the neutral base)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/fetcher.py, applier.py, apply_handlers.py, _attestation.py (rewire; drop vendor imports)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (AST gate for the four modules)

## Testing

- Happy path: `JiraBackend.project` returns the DIG-defaulted project; `query_project` returns
  the configured project; `assert_env_ready()` passes with all three JIRA_* set.
- Failure/edge: `query_project` preserves today's un-defaulted fail-closed behavior when the
  project is unset; `assert_env_ready()` raises naming exactly the missing JIRA_* vars;
  `apply_handlers` catches `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError` when the Jira subclass is raised
  (assignee soft-fail preserved).
- Gate: the new AST neutrality case fails if any of the four modules re-grows an
  `adapters.jira` import (module-level or nested).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `fetcher.py`, `applier.py`, `apply_handlers.py`, `_attestation.py` contain NO
      `adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` import (module-level or function-nested) ‚Äî verified by
      the new AST case in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`.
- [ ] The Backend port exposes `project`, `query_project`, `assert_env_ready()`, and
      `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError`; `JiraBackend` implements them by delegating to
      `resolve_jira_settings`; behavior is identical (DIG default in the applier guard,
      fail-closed fetcher JQL scoping, `_attestation` JIRA_* fail-fast, assignee soft-fail) ‚Äî
      verified by the unit tests named in Testing.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; whole test tree collects with 0 errors.
fields.title: Backend port scalar surface: project scope, env-readiness fail-fast, neutral assignee error (fetcher/applier/apply_handlers/_attestation)
